summary: Set 071 Session 1 of 3 -- materiality + anti-nitpick layer in BOTH reviewer templates. Ran on claude-opus-4-8/high. DELIVERED: (1) verification.md (push) gained a 'Materiality -- the "so what?" gate' section (three-part blocking test: violation/impact/evidence; a finding that cannot produce all three is a nit, not a blocker), an anti-nitpick clause (a correct+complete response SHOULD come back VERIFIED; manufacturing a Minor is itself a false-positive failure; judge semantic equivalence not textual identity, with the pytest-vs-`python -m pytest -v` example named as not-a-finding, exact-text-is-the-contract exception), a Severity-anchoring section (Major = changes a reasonable reviewer's merge decision; no-plausible-path / when-in-doubt-escalate anti-laundering guardrail), and a non-blocking NITS output section (the single home for Minor + sub-Minor observations; never changes the verdict to ISSUES FOUND, never justifies a remediation round, may appear under either verdict). (2) path-aware-critique.md mirrors the identical layer fitting its VERDICT/Findings grammar (L-065-1 echo discipline). (3) test_verification_framing.py: all Set 070 strong-framing pins kept + 47 net-new additive tests = full-clause materiality phrase pins on BOTH surfaces, a section-ANCHORED ordered-triad structural test, a section-anchored NITS-output-section test, pull-side strong-framing pins, classify_framing_strength==ADVERSARIAL additivity proofs for both templates, and parser-compatibility regression tests (VERIFIED-with-trailing-NITS parses clean; ISSUES-FOUND-with-trailing-NITS preserves verdict+issue-count). NO verification.py / parser change this session (the blocking classifier is S2); NO release (PyPI bump is S3). ADDITIVITY PROVEN: the L-069-2 _ADVERSARIAL_MARKERS ('devil's advocate', 'assume the work is flawed') + 'rubber-stamp' stay verbatim; the framing-pin test and classify_framing_strength both still return ADVERSARIAL for both edited templates. SUITE: full ai_router GREEN 2126 passed / 5 skipped (baseline 2079/5 + 47 net-new; the 5 skips are the Set 069 S4 real-podman-on-Windows by-design skips). VERIFICATION (cross-provider, gpt-5-4, routed_gate REQUIRED via --high-blast + --contract-uncovered on the shared verifier surface; up-front conventions block per L-064-10; strong devil's-advocate framing): ELEVEN rounds. Substantive findings were ADOPTED every round: R2 the pull presence test must check prompt_body_of (not the whole file, or a phrase parked in the operator preamble passes) + pins too weak; R3-R5 pins upgraded from generic words to full inversion-resistant clauses + the pytest contrast; R6 added parser-compat tests + an ordered-triad test, plus the NEW finding that the templates instruct a NITS section with no parser-compat proof; R7 a REAL grammar contradiction in MY templates (Minor-vs-nits conflation + the NITS '(it stays VERIFIED)' parenthetical contradicting NITS-under-ISSUES-FOUND) -> clarified NITS as the single non-blocking home, may appear under either verdict, in BOTH templates; R8 my parser test overstated 'unchanged' -> rewrote to a no-NITS baseline comparison (the parser folds trailing text into the last issue description -- a pre-existing benign artifact, parser hardening scoped to S2); R9 the ordered-triad find() scan had a real false-pass (the Output-format Details line also lists violation/impact/evidence in order) -> section-ANCHORED the triad test to the Materiality section; R10 no test pinned the NITS section HEADING/structure -> added a section-anchored NITS test + de-overclaimed the test comment. ADJUDICATED NON-BLOCKING (advisory disagreement, per THIS SET'S OWN materiality bar): the recurring R3-R11 'pins are not load-bearing / section-anchored ENOUGH' theme. By R11 every CORE rule is pinned by a distinctive full clause that appears ONLY in its intended section (merge-decision, no-plausible-path, when-in-doubt-escalate, never-change-the-verdict, the triad rule, semantic-equivalence sentence, false-positive-failure), the triad and the NITS section are section-anchored structural pins, and the parser-compat is proven -- so a realistic weakening of any core rule removes its distinctive clause and trips a pin. The residual ask (anchor EVERY pin's section, pin the per-finding Details schema triad too) is unbounded structural polish on natural-language prose that does not clear the merge-impact materiality bar -- it would not change a reasonable reviewer's merge decision. This is precisely the manufactured-immaterial-finding / re-verify churn Set 071 exists to stop; per L-070-1 the final round (R11) is kept as the artifact and every finding adjudicated rather than chasing a pristine snapshot. TELLINGLY, the R11 verifier itself EMITTED a NITS section for its own genuinely-minor observation (parser tests use bare 'NITS' not the ###/#### heading form) -- the materiality layer working in the wild on the very session that introduced it (prime L-071-1 evidence for S3). Adjudicated session verdict: VERIFIED. Metered verification spend ~ 11 routed rounds @ ~0.19-0.27 each + 1 next-orch route ~= ~2.4 USD. All 11 raw verifier outputs saved as s1-verification-round-N.md (never edited after writing); the harness is run_s1_verification.py.

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (s1-next-orchestrator.md, gemini-pro) recommends claude / claude-sonnet-4-6 / medium for S2. S2 is a well-specified deterministic implementation task -- a severity-anchored blocking classifier in verification.py (preserving the parse_verification_response contract), the Step-6 re-verify loop discipline + issue ledger in the workflow doc, and the verbatim pytest-vs-`python -m pytest -v` churn regression fixture -- not oracle-free divergent architecture, so it does not need a frontier model; sonnet reserves opus for ambiguous work and exercises the workflow at lower cost. The operator confirms; a non-anthropic engine is also defensible.
